title: Issue 137 AI Export Productization Run
subtitle: Inference-time Codex run for productizing AI-enhanced living-doc exports.
scope: GitHub issue #137 in triadflow/living-doc-compositor, following the working vertical slice from issue #135.
sourceCoverage: Hydrated from GitHub issues #137 and #135, registry type contracts, renderer code, fixture data, and current AI export tests.
objective: Productize AI-enhanced living-doc exports after the first working vertical slice from GitHub issue #137. Improve the advisory UX, prompt and payload quality, profile maturity, and export/test quality for the first three convergence types without broad rollout or canonical source mutation.
successCondition: A living-doc export shows the AI advisory layer as a coherent, credible part of the document experience for design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder sections. Standalone export behavior remains intact, the advisory layer remains read-only and document-grounded by default, prompt/payload brittleness is reduced, and tests cover refined UX plus local-model failure/recovery paths.

objectiveFacets.name: Frame objective
objectiveFacets.description: Issue #137 is treated as productization of the existing #135 vertical slice, not a new architecture pass.

objectiveFacets.name: Preserve boundaries
objectiveFacets.description: Standalone export behavior stays intact, advisory hydration remains read-only, and default grounding remains document-only.

objectiveFacets.name: Advisory UX
objectiveFacets.description: The advisory card layout, visible copy, states, and hierarchy feel intentional in rendered documents.

objectiveFacets.name: Prompt and payload quality
objectiveFacets.description: Prompts, output handling, and payload shaping are tightened around real local-model behavior and near-miss JSON.

objectiveFacets.name: Profile maturity
objectiveFacets.description: The first three convergence type profile sets remain the product surface and are named/placed clearly.

objectiveFacets.name: Verification coverage
objectiveFacets.description: Tests cover the refined advisory UX and additional failure or recovery paths without broad rollout.

objectiveFacets.name: Finalize handoff
objectiveFacets.description: The run doc records implementation evidence, residual risk, and readiness states.

invariants.name: Status needs evidence
invariants.statement: Do not mark objectiveReady true unless implementation changes and test evidence are linked in this doc.

invariants.name: Read-only advisory boundary
invariants.statement: Do not introduce runtime behavior that mutates canonical living-doc JSON or replaces Cmd-K AI-pass patch acceptance.

invariants.name: Standalone export contract
invariants.statement: Do not weaken the rendered HTML standalone contract; any AI runtime path must keep file-open readability without JavaScript or a model server.

invariants.name: First three only
invariants.statement: Do not broaden advisory rollout beyond design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder while productizing this issue.

invariants.name: Document grounded default
invariants.statement: Prompts and payloads must assume only the exported document context unless a later source explicitly opts into tool-backed grounding.

callouts.title: Run policy
items: Checkpoint commits are allowed for this run doc and its rendered HTML only.
The worktree contains many pre-existing unrelated edits; source-code changes must be scoped and must not revert user work.
Source-code commits are not assumed unless explicitly requested after implementation.

sections.title: Issue Orbit
sections.rationale: Issue #137 is a follow-on to #135, so the issue relationship is a structural part of the objective rather than background reading.

data.name: #137 - Productize AI-enhanced living-doc exports after the first working vertical slice
data.relevance: Defines the productization goal: improve advisory UX, prompt/payload quality, profile maturity, standalone export quality, and tests for the first three convergence types.

data.name: #135 - Integrate AI Render Graph static inference slots
data.relevance: Specifies the first-three type mapping and acceptance criteria that the current implementation slice appears to follow.

sections.title: Findings
sections.rationale: Ground-truth observations from source hydration that are not themselves code anchors or attempts.

data.name: The working slice is present in the dirty tree
notes: The registry declares aiProfiles for design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder.
The renderer emits section advisory regions, typed task specs, metadata, and a mount script using the local ai-render-graph file runtime.
The fixture enables all three first-slice profile sets and the e2e suite already checks baseline rendering plus local-model shape normalization and malformed JSON retry.

data.name: The issue explicitly keeps advisory hydration read-only and document-grounded
notes: Non-goals include tool-backed search, internet-grounded inference by default, replacing Cmd-K, canonical document mutation, and broad rollout beyond the first three types.
The implementation work must therefore improve the exported reader experience without changing source ownership or grounding assumptions.

data.name: Current advisory UI still reads like an implementation widget
notes: The section uses a technical label, slot ids as visible chips, an all-equal three-card grid, and status copy such as Idle/Ready/Calling local model.
Issue #137 asks specifically for layout, copy, states, hierarchy, and per-profile slot language to feel like part of the living doc.

sections.title: Code Anchors
sections.rationale: Pinned code locations that control the AI advisory export behavior and tests for this issue.

data.name: Registry aiProfiles for the first three convergence types
data.what_it_does: Declares the valid advisory profile ids, names, descriptions, and slot placements for design-code-spec-flow, verification-surface, and proof-ladder.

why_it_matters.text: Profile maturity changes should stay in the registry and renderer type map, not be inferred ad hoc from rendered HTML.

data.name: Renderer AI type specs and prompt builders
data.what_it_does: Normalizes section items, builds prompts, defines output schemas, aliases, and rendered field specs for first-three advisory profiles.

why_it_matters.text: Prompt and payload quality work should land here while preserving document-only grounding.

data.name: Renderer advisory UI
data.what_it_does: Renders the section-level advisory block, advisory task cards, run controls, fallback copy, and CSS.

why_it_matters.text: This is the most direct product surface for issue #137's UX and state-copy requirements.

data.name: Runtime result normalization and retry path
data.what_it_does: Mounts the embedded runtime, calls a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint, coerces near-miss JSON into expected schemas, retries parse-only failures, and updates advisory fields and states.

why_it_matters.text: Issue #137 explicitly asks to reduce brittleness around malformed or near-miss local-model JSON and make success/running/retry/error states intentional.

data.name: AI enhanced fixture and tests
data.what_it_does: Provides deterministic first-three test data, validates embedded spec shape, and exercises rendered advisory UI with mocked local model responses.

why_it_matters.text: The acceptance bar needs new tests for refined UX states and additional failure/recovery paths.

sections.title: Verification Surface
sections.rationale: Evidence targets for deciding whether #137 is actually productized rather than just rendered.

data.name: Rendered advisory UX reads as living-doc product surface
data.currentCoverage: Existing tests assert that advisory regions, profile labels, buttons, fallback text, and task bindings appear.
data.nextStep: Refine advisory card hierarchy, slot copy, fallback language, and state messages; add assertions for the new visible product language.
gaps: No test currently distinguishes reader-facing slot language from raw slot ids.
No test currently checks retry-visible or error-visible copy.
No visual screenshot verification has been run for the refined surface.
notes: The refinement should preserve a dense, document-native product feel rather than adding a separate marketing-like AI panel.

data.name: Failure and recovery coverage
data.currentCoverage: The suite covers common local-model JSON aliases and one malformed JSON retry path.
data.nextStep: Add coverage for task-level error state and/or nested wrapper/list coercion that local models commonly emit.
gaps: No explicit e2e assertion for final surfaced local-model error copy.
No dedicated test for wrapper shapes beyond simple alias handling.
notes: Full npm test may be expensive; targeted contract/e2e tests are acceptable evidence for this run if they cover the changed surface.

data.name: Standalone export contract remains intact
data.currentCoverage: Contract tests validate that rendered HTML includes a schema-valid application/ai-render-graph+json script and metadata.
data.nextStep: Render the AI fixture and run the AI spec contract after modifications.
gaps: Need fresh verification after source changes.
notes: Any change must preserve file-open readability when no model endpoint is available.

sections.title: Attempts
sections.rationale: Concrete actions taken during this run and what each action proved.

data.name: Created governed run doc
data.cost_to_apply: One objective-scoped JSON doc plus rendered HTML checkpoint.

what_tried.text: Read #137, #135, registry contracts, renderer AI code, fixture data, and current tests; scaffolded and hydrated this run doc before source implementation.

what_proved.text: The issue is implementable through renderer UX/state-copy changes plus targeted tests while preserving the #135 architecture boundaries.
